goal: Design Isaac Sim's integration into a Teamcenter + CATIA + Simulink PLM toolchain without disrupting it.
thoughts: Authority stays in PLM: CATIA owns geometry, Teamcenter owns BOM, Simulink owns control. Isaac Sim is a downstream physics consumer. Interchange: STEP/JT -> USD -> Nucleus. Simulink: FMU co-sim if supported, else ROS2 bridge via Simulink ROS toolbox. Round-trip back into CATIA is NOT shipped — flag it. 1 kHz co-sim feasible but Simulink boundary overhead is the real limiter. Never invent a 'Teamcenter connector'.
code:
# Advisory — the flow and real integration points.
# 1. Ground the interchange layer (no Teamcenter connector exists)
lookup_knowledge(query="omniverse step jt importer assembly structure preservation")
lookup_knowledge(query="isaac sim fmu co-simulation support kit version")
lookup_product_spec(product="Omniverse Connectors")

# 2. Forward flow: Teamcenter --STEP/JT--> file share --STEP importer--> USD --> Nucleus
# Preserve hierarchy + stamp BOM metadata into USD attrs (bom:partNumber, bom:revision)
import_robot(
    source="<STEP_OR_JT_PATH>",
    dest_prim="/World/Assembly_<REV>",
    preserve_hierarchy=True,
    metadata_attrs={"bom:partNumber": "<PN>", "bom:revision": "<REV>"},
)

# 3. Control-side co-sim
# Option A: FMU import (Simulink exports controller as FMU; Isaac Sim steps at boundary)
# Option B: ROS2 bridge via Simulink ROS Toolbox (looser coupling, higher latency)

# 4. Return leg — sim results back to PLM as metadata (NOT geometry round-trip)
export_scene_package(
    source_scene="<SIM_RESULT_SCENE>",
    dest="<PLM_INBOX>/sim_report_<RUN_ID>.usdz",
    include_metadata_sidecar=True,
)

# 5. Honest limit — 1 kHz co-sim is boundary-overhead bound, not PhysX bound
lookup_knowledge(query="isaac sim physx substep 1khz co-sim real-time latency")
failure_modes: assistant promises round-trip edits back into CATIA
assistant claims 1 kHz co-sim without caveats
assistant skips the authority-statement (Isaac Sim is consumer, not source)
